item.message: Exceeded skills context budget of 2%. All skill descriptions were removed and 7 additional skills were not included in the model-visible skills list.

item.text: I’ll use the ThreeNative playtest/verification guidance for this benchmark so the implementation stays inside the structured-source flow and has a practical proof path. First I’m copying the starter, then I’ll inspect the project shape and available `tn` commands before editing durable source.

item.aggregated_output:
---
name: threenative-runner-playtest
description: Verify the ThreeNative Crystal Runner example or similar primitive scripted runner gameplay across Three.js web and Bevy native runtimes. Use when asked to playtest runner motion, validate scripted Transform/resource effects, capture multiple web/Bevy screenshots, diagnose player-moving-but-obstacles-static bugs, or prove web/native runner behavior from artifacts.
---

# ThreeNative Runner Playtest

Use this skill in the `threejs-to-bevy` repo for primitive runner examples that
depend on scripted ECS systems moving render entities through `Transform`
patches.

## Build And Contract Checks

Start from the repo root:

```bash
node packages/cli/dist/index.js build --project examples/crystal-runner --json
node packages/cli/dist/index.js validate --project examples/crystal-runner --json
```

If package code changed, build the affected packages first:

```bash
pnpm --filter @threenative/ir build
pnpm --filter @threenative/runtime-web-three build
pnpm --filter @threenative/cli build
```

Inspect the emitted contract:

```bash
jq '{requiredCapabilities, entry, files}' examples/crystal-runner/dist/crystal-runner.bundle/manifest.json
jq '{systems:.systems, schema:.schema}' examples/crystal-runner/dist/crystal-runner.bundle/systems.ir.json
jq '{assets:[.assets[].primitive], assetCount:(.assets|length)}' examples/crystal-runner/dist/crystal-runner.bundle/assets.manifest.json
sed -n '1,220p' examples/crystal-runner/dist/crystal-runner.bundle/scripts.bundle.js
```

Confirm:

- assets are generated primitives only (`box`, `capsule`, etc.)
- manifest capabilities include scripting, input, camera, lights, UI, physics,
  generated primitive meshes, and runtime config
- systems declare `Transform` in `reads`/`writes` when scripts call
  `entity.get("Transform")` or `entity.patch("Transform", ...)`
- `scripts.bundle.js` includes the runner gameplay system and patches obstacle,
  pickup, player, and `RunnerState`

## Web Runtime Playtest

Run the automated verifier:

```bash
node packages/cli/dist/index.js verify --project examples/crystal-runner --frames 4 --expect-motion --json
```

Required evidence:

- status is `pass`
- `checks.nonblank.ok` is true
- `checks.frameDiff.ok` is true with `expectedMotion: true`
- no `pageErrors`
- screenshots exist under `examples/crystal-runner/artifacts/verify/`
- `web-effect-log.json` contains `patch` entries for `Transform` and
  `resource` entries for `RunnerState`

For manual input playtest, start a preview and drive it with Playwright. Save
artifacts under `examples/crystal-runner/artifacts/playtest/`, including:

- `web-00-ready.png`
- `web-01-left.png`
- `web-02-jump.png`
- `web-03-right-running.png`
- `web-04-late.png`
- `web-playtest-report.json`

Use key input (`ArrowLeft`, `Space`, `ArrowRight`) and summarize
`globalThis.__THREENATIVE_EFFECT_LOG__`.

## Bevy Runtime Playtest

Run the native regression that guards the player-moves-but-obstacles-static
class of bug:

```bash
cargo test -p threenative_runtime systems_context_should_include_union_of_declared_query_matches --quiet
```

Then capture multiple native frames:

```bash
for frame in 30 120 240; do
  cargo run --quiet -p threenative_runtime --bin threenative_capture -- \
    /home/joao/projects/threejs-to-bevy/examples/crystal-runner/dist/crystal-runner.bundle \
    camera.main \
    /home/joao/projects/threejs-to-bevy/examples/crystal-runner/artifacts/playtest/bevy-frame-${frame}.png \
    "$frame" || exit 1
done
```

Compare early vs late captures:

```bash
node packages/cli/dist/index.js compare-images \
  examples/crystal-runner/artifacts/playtest/bevy-frame-30.png \
  examples/crystal-runner/artifacts/playtest/bevy-frame-240.png \
  --json
```

Bevy motion is proven when the changed-pixel ratio is above the default
threshold and visual inspection shows cubes/pickups advancing or recycling.

## Common Failure

If the player moves but obstacles/pickups do not in Bevy, inspect:

- `runtime-bevy/crates/threenative_runtime/src/systems_context.rs`
- `runtime-bevy/crates/threenative_runtime/src/systems_host.rs`
- `runtime-bevy/crates/threenative_runtime/src/lib.rs`

The native system context must expose the union of all declared query matches,
not only the first query. Otherwise scripts using `ctx.query({ with:
["Obstacle"] })` see no obstacle entities even though the web runtime works.

## Reporting

Report commands and artifact paths. Include objective metrics:

- web verifier status and `frameDiff.changedPixelRatio`
- web playtest report path and latest transform positions
- Bevy screenshot paths for frames 30, 120, 240
- Bevy compare-images changed-pixel ratio
- native regression test result

item.text: The starter already has a simple arena structure, scripts, UI, and smoke playtests. I’m going to use that as the base and convert the durable scene/script/UI into a checkpoint race instead of introducing a separate runtime path.

item.text: The plan artifact is written under this candidate. The current game is a minimal move-to-goal starter, so I’m reading the structured documents to preserve schema fields and declared system/component contracts while expanding it into the race.

I have enough structure now. I’m going to make a compact top-down race: a blue car, track surface and boundary rails, four ordered checkpoint blocks, a finish marker, HUD text bound to one game-state resource, and a script that handles movement, checkpoint progression, finish, and restart.
I’m checking the script API usage in nearby examples before editing, mainly for resource updates and input actions, so the script declares the right reads/writes and survives extraction.

The script API has `getAxis2`, resource patch/set, and button helpers, which is enough for the race loop. I’m applying focused source edits now: scene geometry/resource state, input axes/actions, system declaration, HUD binding, and the player script.
The first source pass is in. I’m validating now; if the compiler flags unsupported source shape or script declarations, I’ll adjust the owning structured files rather than touching generated output.

item.text: Validation passed, but the build caught a portable-script constraint: exported systems cannot close over module-local constants/helpers. I’m moving those constants into the exported function so the compiler can bundle it.
The script now follows the portable export rule. I’m rebuilding and then I’ll run playtest with the smoke scenario to prove keyboard-driven movement and runtime readiness.

item.text: The build got past script extraction and rejected `GameState` in the component `writes` list; resources are not declared there as components. I’m correcting the system declaration and rebuilding.
The system declaration is corrected. I’m running the same narrow checks again now: authoring validation, build, then playtest.

item.text: Authoring validation and build now pass. I’m going to add a benchmark-specific playtest scenario that drives the car along the checkpoint route and checks movement/runtime diagnostics; then I’ll run it through `tn playtest`.
The playtest runner can assert resource values, so I’m using that to prove the finish state as well as movement. I’m reading the assertion shape briefly, then I’ll add the scenario.

The route scenario will hold D/W/A in sequence long enough to intersect each ordered checkpoint, then assert `GameState.phase === "finished"` and the HUD includes `FINISH`. I’m adding it under `playtests/` so the proof is repeatable.
The finish-path playtest is added. I’m running the existing smoke movement test and the new finish scenario against the built browser runtime.

item.text: The smoke playtest failed because the runtime never reached ready and the player did not move; that points to a runtime diagnostic, not tuning. I’m opening the captured diagnostics/effect log and waiting for the finish scenario result before making the next fix.

item.text: The runtime diagnostic is explicit: the script writes `GameState`, but the system document omitted `resourceWrites`. I’m adding that declaration; this should let the runtime accept the HUD/state updates and proceed normally.
Both failed runs used the old bundle, as expected. I’ve added `resourceWrites`, so I’m rebuilding now and then rerunning the playtests against the new bundle.

item.text: The rebuild passed, but my parallel IR read raced the build and still showed the previous systems file. I’m checking the emitted IR once more, then I’ll rerun playtest.
The emitted IR now declares `GameState` as a resource write. I’m rerunning the movement and finish playtests with stable artifacts.

item.text: The smoke movement proof passes now: KeyD moved the player 6.92 units with no console/network/runtime diagnostics. The full finish-route playtest is still running, so I’m waiting for that result.

The route playtest moved cleanly with no diagnostics, but it overshot my central checkpoints because the headless runtime advances coarse fixed ticks under load. I’m moving the ordered checkpoints onto the route corners so keyboard holds still prove ordered progression reliably.

I’m also removing the filtered checkpoint query from the script and filtering the full entity list in-script. That matches the starter’s broad query pattern and avoids depending on query extraction for a marker-only component.
The durable source is adjusted. I’m rebuilding and rerunning the finish-path proof now.

item.text: The finish-path playtest now passes: it reaches `GameState.phase = finished`, checkpoint 4/4, and HUD text containing `FINISH`, with no diagnostics. I’m rerunning the quick smoke movement proof once against this final bundle.

item.text:
Playable checkpoint-race game is implemented in the candidate directory using durable ThreeNative source.

What’s included:
- Visible player vehicle, track boundary rails, ordered checkpoints, and finish marker.
- WASD/arrow-key movement.
- HUD with checkpoint count, timer, finish text, and `R` retry path.
- Finish state reachable after checkpoint 4/4.

Verified with:
- `tn authoring validate --project . --json` passed.
- `tn build --project . --json` passed.
- `tn playtest --scenario playtests/smoke-movement.playtest.json` passed: player moved `6.92` units, no diagnostics.
- `tn playtest --scenario playtests/checkpoint-race-finish.playtest.json` passed: `GameState.phase = finished`, checkpoint `4`, HUD contains `FINISH`, no diagnostics.
